name: Strapi MCP Server
what_it_does: Strapi MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Strapi headless CMS — querying and managing content types and entries, creating and updating content, managing media, exploring schema, and integrating Strapi's open-source headless CMS capabilities into agent-driven content management, editorial automation, and API-first content workflows.
use_cases: Creating and updating Strapi content entries from content generation agents
Querying content types and entries for editorial workflow agents
Managing media uploads and assets from content operations agents
Exploring Strapi schema and content type definitions from data discovery agents
Bulk content operations for migration from data management agents
Automating content publishing and approval workflows from editorial agents
not_for: Teams using WordPress, Contentful, or Ghost (use their specific MCPs)
Strapi Cloud (hosted) may have different API access requirements
Teams needing Strapi plugin development (use Strapi SDK directly)
best_when: An agent needs to manage content in a Strapi headless CMS — for AI-assisted content creation, automated publishing, or API-driven content operations.
avoid_when: You're using Contentful, Sanity, or WordPress — or if you need to modify Strapi plugin code rather than manage content through the API.

auth.notes: Strapi API tokens (full access, read-only, or custom scope). User JWT tokens for user-context operations. API tokens created in Strapi admin under Settings > API Tokens. Role-based access control via Strapi Users & Permissions plugin.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Strapi Community: free, open source, self-hosted
pricing.notes: Strapi Community Edition is open source (MIT) and free to self-host. Strapi Cloud is a managed hosting option. Community MCP server is open source and free.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: configurable. Strapi Cloud: depends on plan

known_agent_gotchas: Strapi v4 and v5 have significantly different API structures — check version before use
Content type UIDs differ from display names — agents must use API IDs (kebab-case plural)
Population and filtering syntax is Strapi-specific — not standard REST or SQL
Draft and publish states require specific fields — agents must set publishedAt correctly
RBAC permissions must allow API access — public content may not require auth, private does
Community MCP server — Strapi API is well-documented but MCP coverage may be partial
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS requires reverse proxy (self-hosted). API Tokens with read-only or custom scope. RBAC via Users & Permissions plugin. GDPR. Community MCP server. Use read-only API token where possible. Configure HTTPS with reverse proxy.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS via reverse proxy. API Tokens with scope control. RBAC. GDPR. Community MCP server. Use read-only tokens where possible. Configure HTTPS.
